findings.issue: False-green test: 'shows error when acceptInvitation fails after successful redeem' does not seed localStorage.access_token before asserting toBeNull(). The assertion at line 153 is trivially true because nothing was stored. If the localStorage.removeItem('access_token') call at InvitationCompletePage.tsx:90 were accidentally removed, this test would still pass. Every other error-path test in this file properly seeds a stale token first (lines 64, 83, 100, 124), making this an inconsistency in the 'ALL tests' fix.
findings.remediation: Add localStorage.setItem('access_token', 'stale-token') after line 138 (before renderInvitationComplete()) to verify the pre-existing token is actually cleared.

findings.issue: False-green test: 'shows error when handoff_id and app_code are missing' does not seed localStorage.access_token before asserting toBeNull() at line 172. The localStorage.removeItem('access_token') at InvitationCompletePage.tsx:39 is untested against a pre-existing token. Same class of false-green as glm-F1 — other error tests in this file seed the token.
findings.remediation: Add localStorage.setItem('access_token', 'stale-token') before the render() call to verify the pre-existing token is actually cleared.

findings.issue: False-green test: 'shows error when initOAuth fails' does not seed localStorage.access_token before asserting toBeNull() at line 118. The localStorage.removeItem('access_token') at InvitationPage.tsx:60 is untested against a pre-existing token. If the removeItem were deleted, this test would still pass.
findings.remediation: Add localStorage.setItem('access_token', 'stale-token') in the test body before renderInvitationAccept() to verify the pre-existing token is actually cleared.

findings.issue: False-green test: 'oauth callback: handles redeem failure' does not seed localStorage.access_token before asserting toBeNull() at line 346. The localStorage.removeItem('access_token') at AppRouter.tsx:162 is untested against a pre-existing token. The other three OAuth callback error-path tests in this file properly seed it (lines 267, 289, 311), making this an inconsistency.
findings.remediation: Add localStorage.setItem('access_token', 'stale-token') after line 334 to verify the pre-existing token is actually cleared.

findings.issue: Missing assertion: 'login: shows error when initOAuth fails' does not assert localStorage.access_token cleanup at all. The LoginPage catch block calls localStorage.removeItem('access_token') at AppRouter.tsx:58, but the test only asserts sessionStorage cleanup (line 243). The localStorage cleanup for this error path is completely unverified.
findings.remediation: Add expect(localStorage.getItem('access_token')).toBeNull() after line 243, and seed localStorage.setItem('access_token', 'stale-token') before renderWithRouter to make the assertion meaningful.

findings.issue: InvitationPage OAuthButton shows no loading text when loading is true. The button is disabled with disabled:opacity-50, but the label stays static ('Continue with GitHub'). The LoginPage buttons show 'Signing in...' during loading (AppRouter.tsx:83,90). Users on the InvitationPage get no textual feedback during slow initOAuth calls, only a dimmed button.
findings.remediation: Pass a loading prop to OAuthButton and render '{loading ? "Signing in..." : label}' to match the LoginPage pattern.
